704.title: Quick Q&A (Q&A)
704.text:
📌 Quick Questions & Answers (Q&A)
Short and to the point. Expand any section to see the answer.
💡 Didn’t find an answer?
Check the full FAQ or the troubleshooting section.


🚀 Installation & configuration

        
            How do I install MIP?
            
        
        
Clone the repository and build the project:
git clone https://github.com/zshx/mip
cd mip
npm install
npm run build

The builder will suggest adding the binaries folder (./bin) to PATH. Accept it so you can use mip from any folder.
📖 More: Quick start


        
            How do I create a new project?
            
        
        
Run this in an empty folder:
mip init

MIP will create:

mip.json - project configuration
.mip/ - local package storage
README.md with hints

After that, you can install packages:
mip install express
mip run start

📖 Project configuration


        
            Where are installed packages stored?
            
        
        
All packages are stored in .mip/<name>/<version>/. No duplicates in node_modules - only one copy of each version.
Example:
.mip/
├── express/
│   └── 4.18.2/
│       ├── package.json
│       └── ...
└── lodash/
    └── 4.17.21/

📖 Project structure


⚡ Speed & performance

        
            Why is MIP so fast?
            
        
        
MIP uses:

Parallel downloads - up to 10 packages at the same time.
HTTP/2 - request multiplexing.
Aggressive caching - after the first time, packages are stored locally.
No duplication - each package version is stored only once in .mip/.

Benchmarks (cold cache, install express lodash axios):
npm    : 4.2s  (node_modules: 42MB)
pnpm   : 3.1s  (store + symlinks)
mip    : 2.8s  (.mip: 21MB)

Second run (warm cache):
npm    : 1.8s
pnpm   : 0.9s
mip    : 0.4s  ← 4.5x faster than npm!

📖 Benchmarks


        
            How does MIP save disk space?
            
        
        
Unlike npm (which duplicates packages in every project), MIP stores each package version only once in .mip/. If you have 10 projects using express@4.18.2, it will be downloaded and extracted only once.
Comparison:

npm: node_modules/ takes 42 MB per project.
MIP: .mip/ takes 21 MB for all projects (in current versions it may be local; future global caching is possible).

📖 Cache management


🔧 Using commands

        
            What’s the difference between mip run and mip exec?
            
        
        

mip run <script> - runs a script from the scripts section in mip.json. This is similar to npm run.
mip exec <command> - executes any binary from installed packages, even if it’s not listed in scripts. For example: mip exec jest will run Jest if it’s installed.

📖 Commands and exec


        
            How do I install a package globally?
            
        
        
mip install -g <package>
# or
mip global install <package>

Global packages are placed in ~/.mip/global/ and added to PATH. MIP will automatically add the path to .bashrc / .zshrc.
📖 Global packages


        
            How can I see why a specific package is installed?
            
        
        
mip why <package>

This command shows which packages depend on the one you specified. It helps you understand why it’s needed and whether you can remove it.
📖 Dependency analysis


🛡️ CI/CD & security

        
            How do I use MIP in CI?
            
        
        
mip ci

This command installs dependencies strictly according to mip-lock.json, ignoring mip.json. This guarantees a reproducible build.
For extra strictness:
mip ci --frozen-lockfile

It checks that the lockfile matches mip.json - if not, it exits with an error.
📖 CI


        
            How do I check dependency security?
            
        
        
mip audit

It scans all packages from the lockfile for known vulnerabilities (data from the GitHub Advisory Database). It reports severity and suggests fixes:
mip audit --fix   # automatically updates vulnerable packages to safe versions

📖 Security audit


❓ General questions

        
            Where is node_modules?
            
        
        
With MIP, there is no traditional node_modules folder. Instead, everything lives in .mip/. Binaries are run through mip exec or mip run, which look them up inside .mip/.
Why this way?
It eliminates duplication, keeps the structure simple, and makes installs predictable.



        
            Can I work offline?
            
        
        
Yes, if packages were installed at least once already (they are stored in .mip/), MIP can use them without network access. The lockfile also lets you reproduce the exact structure.
📖 Working offline


        
            How is MIP better than npm/pnpm/yarn?
            
        
        



Feature
npm
pnpm
yarn
mip




No duplicates in node_modules
❌
✅

❌

✅

10k+

713.title: MIP Configuration
713.text:
MIP Configuration ⚙️
MIP uses the mip.yml file to store project configuration. Here is a complete reference for all fields.

📁 The mip.yml file
Created automatically with mip init. Can be edited manually.
Full example
name: my-project
version: 1.0.0
language: en

dependencies:
  express: ^4.18.0
  lodash: ^4.17.21

devDependencies:
  jest: ^29.7.0
  eslint: ^8.56.0

scripts:
  start: node index.js
  dev: nodemon index.js
  test: jest
  build: webpack --mode production

workspaces:
  - packages/*
  - apps/*

plugins:
  logger:
    enabled: true
    logFile: mip.log
  notifier:
    enabled: false
    webhook: https://example.com/webhook


📋 Fields
name - project name
Type: string
Required: yes
Example: my-project
The project name. Used in README and when creating plugins.

version - project version
Type: string
Required: yes
Example: 1.0.0
Project version in semver format.

language - interface language
Type: string
Required: no (default: en)
Example: ru
The language for MIP messages. Available values: en, ru, es, fr, de, it, pt, zh, ja, ko.
mip language ru


dependencies - production dependencies
Type: object
Required: no
Example: express: ^4.18.0
Packages required for the application to run.

devDependencies - development dependencies
Type: object
Required: no
Example: jest: ^29.7.0
Packages only needed for development (tests, linters, builds).

scripts - scripts
Type: object
Required: no
Example: start: node index.js
Custom scripts. Run via mip run <name>.
Common scripts:

start - start the application
dev - development mode (with reload)
test - run tests
build - build the project
lint - lint the code


workspaces - monorepositories
Type: array of strings
Required: no
Example: ["packages/*", "apps/*"]
A list of patterns for finding workspaces (monorepositories). Supports *.
See: mip workspaces.

plugins - plugin configuration
Type: object
Required: no
Example: logger: { enabled: true, logFile: "mip.log" }
Configuration for MIP plugins. Each key is the plugin name, the value is its settings.
Standard fields:

enabled - whether the plugin is enabled
Other fields - specific to each plugin


🔧 Adding dependencies
Manually
Add to mip.yml:
dependencies:
  axios: ^1.6.0

Via CLI
mip install axios

Save to devDependencies
mip install jest --save-dev

Without saving
mip install nodemon --no-save


📜 Adding scripts
Manually
scripts:
  start: node index.js
  dev: nodemon index.js

Run
mip run start
mip run dev


🌍 Changing language
Via CLI
mip language ru

Manually
language: ru

Available languages



Code
Language




en
English


ru
Русский


es
Español


fr
Français


de
Deutsch


it
Italiano


pt
Português


zh
中文


ja
日本語


ko
한국어




🧩 Workspace configuration
Adding
workspaces:
  - packages/*
  - apps/*
  - libs/shared

Supported patterns



Pattern
Description




packages/*
All folders inside packages/


apps/*
All folders inside apps/


libs/shared
A specific folder



Commands
mip workspaces list
mip workspaces run test
mip workspaces install


🔌 Plugin configuration
Adding config
plugins:
  logger:
    enabled: true
    logFile: mip.log
  notifier:
    enabled: false
    webhook: https://example.com/webhook

Enable/disable
mip plugin enable logger
mip plugin disable notifier


🔄 Migration from mip.json or package.json
On first run, MIP automatically migrates old configs:
# Before
mip.json
mip-lock.json

# After
mip.yml          # with mip.json.backup
mip-lock.yml     # with mip-lock.json.backup

Old files are saved as .backup for rollback. (Feature is in beta)

💡 Best practices

Always commit mip-lock.yml - ensures reproducibility
Use --save-dev - for development tools
Don’t commit .mip/ - add it to .gitignore
Use specific versions in mip-lock.yml
Update language - if you need translations
Add comments in mip.yml - YAML allows it!


🔗 Related pages

mip init - creating mip.yml
mip install - adding dependencies
mip run - running scripts
mip workspaces - monorepositories
mip language - changing language

748.title: mip features - behavior customization
748.text:
mip features
What is this?
MIP has a built-in feature system that lets you control almost every aspect of its behavior. From installation speed to security checks, you can enable or disable features with a single command.

Quick start
# Generate default configuration
mip --genconfig

# View all available features
mip --list-features

# Enable a feature
mip config set features.install.parallel true

# Disable a feature
mip config set features.update.checkForUpdates false

# Disable ALL features at once
mip config set features.config.enabled false

How it works

Features are stored in mip.config.yml (project-level) or ~/.mip/config.yml (global)
Each feature has a default value - you only need to override what you want to change
Master switch - config.enabled: false disables ALL feature modifications
Zero performance impact - features are loaded once at startup
Granular control - enable/disable specific behaviors without affecting others

This section will help you install MIP and set up your first project.

Section
Description




Quick start
Install and create your first project in 5 minutes

All mip.yml settings




🚀 Quick start
1. Install MIP
git clone https://github.com/kiwinatra/mip
cd mip
npm install
npm run build

2. Create a project
mip init

MIP will create:

mip.yml - project configuration in YAML

3. Install dependencies
mip install express

4. Run the project
mip run start


📁 Project structure
After mip init, you will see:

├── mip.yml          # Project configuration (YAML)
├── mip-lock.yml     # Locked package versions (YAML)
├── .mip/
│   ├── manifest.json # Installed packages manifest
│   ├── cache/        # Download cache
│   └── packages/     # Local packages
└── node_modules/     # Symlinks to the global cache


🔧 Main commands
# Initialization
mip init

# Install packages
mip install <package>

# Run scripts
mip run <script>

# List packages
mip list

# Update packages
mip update

# Remove packages
mip uninstall <package>

# Security audit
mip audit


🌍 Switching language
mip language ru

Available languages: en, ru, es, fr, de, it, pt, zh, ja, ko.

784.title: Quick Start
784.text:
Quick Start 🚀
Install MIP and create your first project in 5 minutes.

📦 Installing MIP
1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/kiwinatra/mip
cd mip

2. Install dependencies
npm install

3. Build the project
npm run build

4. Add to PATH (optional)
The builder will offer to add the binary folder to PATH:
export PATH="$PATH:$(pwd)/bin"

Or add to ~/.zshrc / ~/.bashrc:
echo 'export PATH="$PATH:~/Desktop/mip/bin"' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc

Now mip is available from any folder.

🏗️ Creating a project
1. Create a project folder
mkdir my-project
cd my-project

2. Initialize the project
mip init

MIP will create:
my-project/
├── mip.yml          # Project configuration in YAML
├── .mip/            # Cache and manifest directory
└── README.md        # Documentation

💡 What is mip.yml?
mip.yml is the new YAML configuration format. It’s easier to read, supports comments, and is more pleasant to edit manually than JSON.

3. View mip.yml
name: my-project
version: 1.0.0
language: en
dependencies: {}
devDependencies: {}
scripts: {}
workspaces: []


📦 Installing packages
Install Express
mip install express

Output:
📦 Installing express...
✅ express installed

Check mip.yml
dependencies:
  express: ^4.18.0

Install a development package
mip install nodemon --save-dev

Install everything from mip.yml
mip install


📜 Scripts
Add scripts to mip.yml
scripts:
  start: node index.js
  dev: nodemon index.js

Run a script
mip run start
mip run dev


📂 Project structure
my-project/
├── mip.yml          # YAML configuration
├── mip-lock.yml     # Pinned versions
├── .mip/
│   ├── manifest.json # Installed packages manifest
│   ├── cache/        # Download cache
│   └── packages/     # Local packages
└── node_modules/     # Symlinks to global cache


🧪 Verification
View installed packages
mip list

Check for outdated packages
mip outdated

Security audit
mip audit


🗑️ Cleaning
Clear cache
mip cache clean

Clear global cache
mip cache clean --global

Remove a package
mip uninstall express


🎉 Done!
You’ve installed MIP and created your first project. Next:

Configuration - configuring mip.yml
Commands - all MIP commands
Plugins - extending MIP


🔗 Related pages

Installation - full installation guide
Configuration - project setup
mip init - creating a project
mip install - installing packages

814.title: Installation Errors
814.text:
Installation Errors 📦
The most common issues that occur when installing packages and how to solve them.

🔍 General diagnostics
Check the environment
mip doctor

Check the lockfile
cat mip-lock.yml

Check the cache
mip cache size
mip cache size --global


🐛 Common errors
1. mip-lock.yml not found
Error:
❌ Lockfile not found. Run mip install first

Cause: The lockfile is missing or was deleted.
Solution:
mip install


2. Package not found in registry
Error:
❌ Package not found: some-package

Cause: The package does not exist in the registry.
Solution:
# Check the name
mip search some-package

# Verify the package exists
mip info some-package


3. Version not found
Error:
❌ Version not found: some-package@99.99.99

Cause: A non-existent version was specified.
Solution:
# View available versions
mip info some-package


4. Permission denied
Error:
❌ EACCES: permission denied

Cause: No write permissions for .mip/ or node_modules/.
Solution:
# Fix permissions
sudo chown -R $(whoami) .

# Or delete and recreate
rm -rf node_modules .mip
mip install


5. SSL error
Error:
❌ UNABLE_TO_VERIFY_LEAF_SIGNATURE

Cause: SSL certificate issues.
Solution:
# Temporarily disable strict verification
NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 mip install

# Or update Node.js


6. Package already installed
Error:
✅ Package already installed

This is not an error. The package is already there.
Solution:
# Force reinstall
mip install lodash --force


7. No free space
Error:
❌ ENOSPC: no space left on device

Cause: Disk space is full.
Solution:
# Clear cache
mip cache clean
mip cache clean --global

# Remove old packages
mip uninstall <package>

# Check size
mip cache size
mip cache size --global


8. Dependency conflict
Error:
❌ Peer dependency conflict

Cause: The package requires a different version of a dependency.
Solution:
# Install the required version
mip install lodash@4.17.20

# Or update all packages
mip update


🧹 Cleaning and reinstalling
Full reinstall
# Clean everything
rm -rf node_modules .mip
rm -f mip-lock.yml
mip cache clean

# Install again
mip install

Cache only
mip cache clean
mip install

Lockfile only
rm -f mip-lock.yml
mip install


🛠️ Debugging
Enable debug mode
DEBUG=1 mip install lodash

Check logs
# Logs are printed to the console
# For logger plugins:
cat mip.log

Check versions
mip --version
node --version


🔗 Related pages

Network & Registries - network issues
Lock Files - lockfile issues
mip doctor - environment diagnostics
mip cache - cache management

862.title: Lock File Issues 🔒
862.text:
---
title: "Lock File Issues"
description: "Problems with mip-lock.yml and how to solve them."
---

# Lock File Issues 🔒

`mip-lock.yml` is an important file that pins exact dependency versions. If it is corrupted or out of sync, problems can arise.

---

## 🔍 What is a lockfile?

`mip-lock.yml` is a file where MIP stores:

- Exact versions of all installed packages
- Tarball URLs
- SHA256 hashes for integrity verification
- The dependency tree

**Example:**

```yaml
version: 1.0.0
packages:
  lodash@4.17.21:
    version: 4.17.21
    resolved: https://registry.npmjs.org/lodash/-/lodash-4.17.21.tgz
    integrity: sha512-...
    dependencies: {}


🐛 Common issues
1. Lockfile not found
Error:
❌ Lockfile not found. Run mip install first

Cause: mip-lock.yml is missing or was deleted.
Solution:
# Create a lockfile
mip install


2. Lockfile corrupted
Error:
❌ Failed to parse lockfile
YAMLException: ...

Cause: The file is corrupted or contains invalid YAML.
Solution:
# Check the content
cat mip-lock.yml

# Delete and recreate
rm -f mip-lock.yml
mip install


3. --frozen-lockfile error
Error:
❌ Frozen lockfile check failed:
  • lodash: missing from lockfile
  • axios: extra in lockfile
💡 Run 'mip install' to update lockfile

Cause: mip.yml was changed but the lockfile was not updated.
Solution:
# Update the lockfile
mip install

# Commit the changes
git add mip-lock.yml
git commit -m "chore: update lockfile"


4. Version conflict in lockfile
Error:
❌ Version conflict in lockfile

Cause: The lockfile has conflicting versions of the same package.
Solution:
# Deduplicate
mip dedupe

# Or reinstall
rm -rf .mip node_modules
rm -f mip-lock.yml
mip install


5. Lockfile out of sync with .mip/
Error:
⚠️ Missing packages: lodash

Cause: A package is in the lockfile but missing from .mip/.
Solution:
# Reinstall all packages
mip install


🛠️ Recovery
Full recovery
# Delete everything
rm -rf .mip node_modules
rm -f mip-lock.yml

# Install again
mip install

# Verify
mip list

Update lockfile
# Update lockfile without installing
mip genlock

Deduplicate
# Remove duplicate versions
mip dedupe


🔬 Lockfile verification
View content
cat mip-lock.yml | grep -E '^  [a-z]'

Check integrity
# Check SHA256 hashes
mip doctor

Compare with mip.yml
# Check synchronization
mip ci --frozen-lockfile


🧹 Cleaning
Delete lockfile
rm -f mip-lock.yml

Recreate lockfile
mip install

Clean and recreate
rm -f mip-lock.yml
rm -rf .mip
mip install


💡 Best practices

Always commit mip-lock.yml - ensures reproducibility
Don’t edit the lockfile manually - use MIP commands
Use mip ci --frozen-lockfile in CI - checks synchronization
Run mip dedupe periodically - removes duplicates
Keep the lockfile in the repository - speeds up installation


🔗 Related pages

mip genlock - lockfile generation
mip ci - install from lockfile
mip dedupe - deduplication
mip doctor - diagnostics

883.title: Network & Registries
883.text:
Network & Registries 🌐
Network issues are among the most common problems when working with package managers. Here’s how to fix them in MIP.

🔍 Diagnostics
Check internet
# Check registry availability
curl -I https://registry.npmjs.org/

# Check DNS
nslookup registry.npmjs.org

# Check ping
ping -c 4 registry.npmjs.org

Check in MIP
# Environment diagnostics
mip doctor


🐛 Common problems
1. Cannot connect to registry
Error:
❌ Failed to fetch: https://registry.npmjs.org/
Error: connect ETIMEDOUT

Cause: Internet issues or the registry is unavailable.
Solution:
# Check internet
ping 8.8.8.8

# Check registry
curl -I https://registry.npmjs.org/

# Try again later


2. Slow download speed
Symptom: Packages download very slowly.
Cause: Slow internet, DNS issues, or region.
Solution:
# Use a mirror
mip install lodash --registry https://registry.npmmirror.com

# Or specify another registry in config


3. SSL certificate error
Error:
❌ UNABLE_TO_VERIFY_LEAF_SIGNATURE

Cause: SSL certificate problems.
Solution:
# Temporarily disable verification (not for production!)
NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 mip install

# Or update Node.js


4. Rate limit exceeded
Error:
❌ 429 Too Many Requests

Cause: Too many requests to the registry.
Solution:
# Use GITHUB_TOKEN for GitHub API
export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxx

# Or use cache
mip install --offline


5. Cannot connect to GitHub API
Error:
❌ GitHub API request failed: 403

Cause: GitHub API rate limit exceeded.
Solution:
# Set a token
export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxx

# Or use a different registry
mip install lodash --registry https://registry.npmjs.org/


6. Proxy
Symptom: Requests don’t work due to a corporate proxy.
Cause: Proxy is not configured.
Solution:
# Configure proxy
export HTTP_PROXY=http://proxy.company.com:8080
export HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.company.com:8080

# Now mip will use the proxy


🔧 Registry configuration
Use a different registry
# Specify a registry for a specific command
mip install lodash --registry https://registry.npmmirror.com

# Specify a registry in config
# In mip.yml:
registry: https://registry.npmmirror.com

Popular registries



Registry
URL
Region




npm (official)
https://registry.npmjs.org/
Global


npmmirror
https://registry.npmmirror.com
China




🌐 Offline mode
Work without internet
After the first installation, MIP can work offline:
mip install --offline

Caching
MIP caches packages in ~/.mip/store/. If a package is already in the cache, it will not be downloaded again.

🔍 Network debugging
Enable logs
# Enable debug
DEBUG=1 mip install lodash

Check connection
# Check registry
curl -v https://registry.npmjs.org/lodash

# Check GitHub API
curl -v https://api.github.com/repos/kiwinatra/mip


🛠️ Quick fixes
Everything broken due to network
# Try again later
# Or use a different registry
mip install lodash --registry https://registry.npmmirror.com

# Or use cache
mip install --offline

Proxy
export HTTP_PROXY=http://proxy.company.com:8080
export HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.company.com:8080
mip install lodash

GitHub token
export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxx
mip repo kiwinatra/mip
